name: Band Overhead Press
resistance_profile: Band tension climbs as you press, so the lockout (shortened position) is the hardest point — an ascending resistance curve with little load at the bottom.
cues: Stand tall and brace before pressing
Drive all the way to a locked-out top
Resist the band on the way down, do not let it snap back
execution_steps: Stand on the middle of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold a handle or the band in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Brace the trunk, then press both hands overhead until the elbows lock out.
Squeeze the delts at the top where the band tension is highest.
Lower under control to shoulder height and repeat.
good_when: Training at home, travelling, or warming up the shoulders
Joints feel better with light load at the stretch and more at the top
High-rep pump work with minimal joint stress
bad_when: You need heavy overload for strength progression
You want the delt loaded hardest at the stretch
The band is too weak to challenge your target reps
common_errors: Letting the band yank the arms down fast
Overarching the lower back to press
Using a band far too light to reach failure
progressions: Use a thicker band or double it up
Add reps, then add a pause at lockout
regressions: Seated band press with back support
